subject: BCC runtime compilation
old: BCC's built-in Clang compiler that compiles BPF code at runtime
truth: Stop using BCC's runtime compilation approach; migrate to libbpf with BPF CO-RE and pre-compile BPF code.
quote: BCC provided a built-in Clang compiler that could compile your BPF code in runtime, tailoring it to a specific target kernel on the host.

from_fact: bcc's built-in clang compiler that compiles bpf code at runtime

subject: BCC auto-generated structs for tracepoints
old: BCC's auto-generated structs and naming conventions for tracepoints
truth: Stop relying on BCC's auto-generated structs and naming conventions for tracepoints; use libbpf's explicit struct definitions instead.
quote: You have to remember naming conventions and auto-generated structs for tracepoints.

from_fact: bcc's auto-generated structs and naming conventions for tracepoints

subject: BCC code rewriting for kprobe arguments and kernel data
old: BCC's code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments
truth: Stop relying on BCC's automatic code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments; use libbpf's CO-RE access macros instead.
quote: You have to rely on code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments.

from_fact: bcc's code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments

subject: BCC semi-object-oriented C for BPF maps
old: BCC's semi-object-oriented C code style for working with BPF maps
truth: Stop using BCC's semi-object-oriented C API for BPF maps; use libbpf's direct map access that matches kernel behavior.
quote: You'll write a semi-object-oriented C code when working with BPF maps, which doesn't exactly match what really happens in the kernel.

from_fact: bcc's semi-object-oriented c code style for working with bpf maps

subject: BCC user-space boilerplate for setting up BPF programs
old: BCC requiring manual boilerplate in user-space to set up BPF programs
truth: Stop writing manual boilerplate user-space code for setting up BPF programs; use libbpf's BPF skeletons instead.
quote: BCC will still make you write a bunch of boilerplate code in user-space part of your application, setting up most trivial pieces by hand.

from_fact: bcc requiring manual boilerplate in user-space to set up bpf programs

subject: BCC dependency on kernel headers package
old: BCC requiring kernel headers package installed on every target host
truth: Stop depending on kernel headers package being installed on every target host; use libbpf CO-RE which does not require kernel headers.
quote: dependency on kernel headers package, that has to be installed on every target host.

from_fact: bcc requiring kernel headers package installed on every target host

subject: BCC runtime detection of compilation errors
old: BCC detecting compilation errors only at runtime after restarting the application
truth: Stop relying on runtime-only detection of BPF compilation errors; use libbpf's pre-compilation which catches errors at build time.
quote: even a trivial compilation-time errors are detected only during the runtime, after you've rebuilt and restarted your user-space application completely

from_fact: bcc detecting compilation errors only at runtime after restarting the application

subject: locked memory limit handling
old: BCC unconditionally sets locked memory limit to infinity
new: libbpf does not automatically set locked memory limit
truth: You must manually increase the locked memory limit (e.g. via ulimit) when using libbpf, because libbpf no longer does this automatically.
quote: BCC unconditionally sets this limit to infinity, but libbpf doesn’t do this automatically (by design).

from_fact: bcc unconditionally sets locked memory limit to infinity
libbpf does not automatically set locked memory limit

subject: libbpf print/log callback function signature
old: libbpf_set_print(print_libbpf_log) with function returning int and taking (enum libbpf_print_level lvl, const char *fmt, va_list args)
new: libbpf_set_print callback now returns void instead of int
truth: Stop using a libbpf print callback that returns int; the callback must now return void.
quote: int print_libbpf_log(enum libbpf_print_level lvl, const char *fmt, va_list args)

from_fact: libbpf_set_print(print_libbpf_log) with function returning int and taking (enum libbpf_print_level lvl, const char *fmt, va_list args)
libbpf_set_print callback now returns void instead of int

subject: BPF map declaration macros
old: BCC macros like BPF_ARRAY, BPF_HASH, BPF_PERCPU_ARRAY
truth: Stop using BCC map macros (BPF_ARRAY, BPF_HASH, BPF_PERCPU_ARRAY) and use libbpf struct definitions with SEC(".maps") instead.
quote: The way that BCC and libbpf define BPF maps declaratively is different

from_fact: bcc macros like bpf_array, bpf_hash, bpf_percpu_array

subject: kernel header includes
truth: Stop including individual kernel headers and include vmlinux.h plus bpf_helpers.h, bpf_core_read.h, and bpf_tracing.h instead.
quote: BCC's implicit `#include <linux/...>` kernel headers are replaced by a single `#include "vmlinux.h"`.

subject: field access syntax
truth: Stop using direct C pointer-chaining field accesses like tsk->parent->pid and use BPF_CORE_READ macro instead.
quote: BCC silently rewrites your BPF code and turns field accesses like tsk->parent->pid into a series of bpf_probe_read() calls. Libbpf/BPF CO-RE doesn't have such a luxury

subject: BPF map default size
new: libbpf requires explicit map size specification
truth: You must specify map size explicitly in libbpf; there is no default like BCC's 10240.
quote: Pay attention to the default size of maps in BCC, it’s usually 10240. With libbpf you have to specify the size explicitly.

from_fact: libbpf requires explicit map size specification

subject: BPF map operations syntax
new: libbpf helper function calls (bpf_map_operation_elem(&some_map, some, args))
truth: Replace BCC's pseudo-C++ map method calls (e.g. .lookup(), .update()) with libbpf helper functions (e.g. bpf_map_lookup_elem(), bpf_map_update_elem()).
quote: some_map.operation(some, args)

from_fact: libbpf helper function calls (bpf_map_operation_elem(&some_map, some, args))

subject: perf_submit call
truth: Replace .perf_submit() with bpf_perf_event_output() and add BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU flag.
quote: events.perf_submit(args, data, data_len);

subject: BPF program section naming
old: BCC auto-detects program type without SEC()
new: libbpf requires SEC() macro with specific section name
truth: All BPF programs must be annotated with SEC("section_name") macro in libbpf; BCC does not require this.
quote: All functions representing BPF programs need to be marked with custom section name through use of SEC() macro

from_fact: bcc auto-detects program type without sec()
libbpf requires sec() macro with specific section name

subject: tracepoint context type naming
truth: Tracepoint context type in libbpf is trace_event_raw_<name> instead of BCC's tracepoint__<category>__<name> pattern.
quote: name of tracepoint context type.

subject: variable-length string field in tracepoints
truth: Replace data_loc_ with __data_loc_ (double underscore prefix) for variable-length string fields in tracepoint context.
quote: data_loc_ becomes __data_loc_ (notice double underscores).

subject: specialized map BTF support
old: BCC may handle key/value types implicitly
new: libbpf requires explicit key_size/value_size for PERF_EVENT_ARRAY, STACK_TRACE, DEVMAP, CPUMAP
truth: PERF_EVENT_ARRAY, STACK_TRACE, DEVMAP, CPUMAP maps don't support BTF types for key/value in libbpf; you must specify key_size and value_size directly.
quote: PERF_EVENT_ARRAY, STACK_TRACE and few other specialized maps (DEVMAP, CPUMAP, etc) don’t support (yet) BTF types for key/value, so specify key_size/value_size directly

from_fact: bcc may handle key/value types implicitly
libbpf requires explicit key_size/value_size for perf_event_array, stack_trace, devmap, cpumap

subject: syscall kprobe names
truth: Starting from kernel 4.17, syscall kprobe names changed from `sys_<name>` to `__x64_sys_<name>` (on x64) and similar architecture-specific prefixes on other platforms.
quote: Syscall functions got renamed in 4.17 kernels. Starting from 4.17 version, syscall kprobe that used to be called, say, `sys_kill`, is called now `__x64_sys_kill` (on x64 systems, other architectures will have different prefix, of course). You’ll have to account for that when trying to attach a kprobe/kretprobe.

subject: global variable initialization requirement
old: global variables could be left uninitialized
new: global variables must be initialized (even to zero)
truth: Global variables must be initialized, otherwise libbpf will decline to load the BPF application.
quote: your variables have to be initialized, otherwise libbpf will decline to load the BPF application.

from_fact: global variables could be left uninitialized
global variables must be initialized (even to zero)

subject: const volatile requirement for read-only globals
old: read-only globals could be declared without const volatile
new: read-only globals must use const volatile to prevent compiler optimizations
truth: Read-only global variables must be declared with `const volatile` to prevent the compiler from erroneously assuming zero values and inlining them.
quote: `const volatile` has to be specified to prevent too clever compiler optimizations (compiler might and will erroneously assume zero values and inline them in code);

from_fact: read-only globals could be declared without const volatile
read-only globals must use const volatile to prevent compiler optimizations

subject: mutable globals must not be static
old: mutable globals could be declared static
new: mutable globals must not be marked as static
truth: Mutable (non-const) global variables must not be marked as `static` for best compiler interoperability.
quote: if you are defining a mutable (non-`const`) variable, make sure they are not marked as `static`: non-static globals interoperate with compiler the best.

from_fact: mutable globals could be declared static
mutable globals must not be marked as static

subject: global variables in BPF
old: Declaring the same global variable in BPF code and user-space code connects them
new: Declaring the same global variable in BPF code and user-space code creates independent, unconnected variables
truth: Stop declaring shared global variables in both BPF and user-space code; use skel->rodata, skel->bss, or skel->data members of the BPF skeleton instead.
quote: declaring exactly the same global variable in BPF code and user-space code will declare completely independent variables, which won't be connected in any way.

from_fact: declaring the same global variable in bpf code and user-space code connects them
declaring the same global variable in bpf code and user-space code creates independent, unconnected variables
associative: share variable between bpf and userspace

subject: loop unrolling requirement
old: Loops in BPF code could be used without explicit unrolling pragma on older kernels
new: Loops in BPF code must be marked with #pragma unroll unless targeting 5.3+ kernel
truth: Stop writing loops in BPF code without #pragma unroll; add #pragma unroll before every loop or target kernel 5.3+.
quote: Unless you are targeting 5.3+ kernel, all the loops in your BPF code have to be marked with #pragma unroll to force Clang to unroll them

from_fact: loops in bpf code could be used without explicit unrolling pragma on older kernels
loops in bpf code must be marked with #pragma unroll unless targeting 5.3+ kernel

subject: static helper functions in BPF
old: static functions in BPF code must be marked __always_inline
new: static functions can drop __always_inline (kernel 4.16+); consider using __noinline instead
truth: Stop marking all BPF static helper functions as __always_inline; drop __always_inline or use __noinline for better code generation on kernels 4.16+.
quote: So feel free to drop __always_inline. You might even consider enforcing no inlining with __noinline

from_fact: static functions in bpf code must be marked __always_inline
static functions can drop __always_inline (kernel 4.16+); consider using __noinline instead

subject: BPF_HASH macro
new: explicit struct with __uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH) annotated SEC(".maps")
truth: Stop using BPF_HASH macro; define an explicit struct with __uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH) inside SEC(".maps") instead.
quote: The `BPF_HASH(name, ktype, vtype)` map macro is replaced by an explicit struct with `__uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH)` annotated `SEC(".maps")`.

from_fact: explicit struct with __uint(type, bpf_map_type_hash) annotated sec(".maps")

subject: BPF_ARRAY macro
truth: Stop using BPF_ARRAY macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY struct inside SEC(".maps") instead.
quote: The `BPF_ARRAY(name, type, size)` macro is replaced by an explicit `BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY` struct annotated `SEC(".maps")`.

subject: BPF_PERF_OUTPUT macro
truth: Stop using BPF_PERF_OUTPUT macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY struct inside SEC(".maps") instead.
quote: The `BPF_PERF_OUTPUT(name)` macro is replaced by an explicit `BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY` struct annotated `SEC(".maps")`.

subject: map.lookup method
truth: Stop using map.lookup(&key); call bpf_map_lookup_elem(&map, &key) instead.
quote: The `map.lookup(&key)` method is replaced by `bpf_map_lookup_elem(&map, &key)`.

subject: map.update method
truth: Stop using map.update(&key, &val); call bpf_map_update_elem(&map, &key, &val, 0) instead.
quote: The `map.update(&key, &val)` method is replaced by `bpf_map_update_elem(&map, &key, &val, 0)`.

subject: events.perf_submit method
truth: Stop using events.perf_submit(ctx, data, len); call bpf_perf_event_output(ctx, &events, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU, data, len) instead.
quote: The `events.perf_submit(ctx, data, len)` method is replaced by `bpf_perf_event_output(ctx, &events, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU, data, len)`.

subject: BCC implicit kernel headers
truth: Stop relying on BCC's implicit kernel headers; include a single #include "vmlinux.h" instead.
quote: BCC's implicit `#include <linux/...>` kernel headers are replaced by a single `#include "vmlinux.h"`.

subject: libbpf required includes
truth: Stop writing BCC programs without includes; libbpf requires explicit #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> and #include <bpf/bpf_core_read.h>.
quote: libbpf requires explicit `#include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h>` and `#include <bpf/bpf_core_read.h>` (BCC needed none).

subject: kernel struct field access
truth: Stop directly accessing kernel struct fields with ptr->field->subfield; use BPF_CORE_READ(ptr, field, subfield) instead.
quote: Direct kernel struct field access `ptr->field->subfield` is replaced by `BPF_CORE_READ(ptr, field, subfield)`.

subject: BCC kprobe function form
truth: Stop using int kprobe__func(struct pt_regs *ctx, args); use SEC("kprobe/func") int BPF_KPROBE(kprobe__func, args) instead.
quote: The BCC kprobe form `int kprobe__func(struct pt_regs *ctx, args)` is replaced by `SEC("kprobe/func") int BPF_KPROBE(kprobe__func, args)`.

subject: BCC implicit section naming
new: explicit SEC("...") annotation on every program
truth: Stop relying on BCC's implicit section naming; add an explicit SEC("...") annotation on every BPF program.
quote: BCC's implicit section naming is replaced by an explicit `SEC("...")` annotation on every program.

from_fact: explicit sec("...") annotation on every program

subject: BCC compile-time #define config substitution
truth: Stop using compile-time #define for config values; use global const volatile variables instead.
quote: BCC's compile-time `#define` config substitution is replaced by global `const volatile` variables.

subject: BCC runtime compilation
new: generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach()
truth: Stop using BCC's runtime compilation; use the generated skeleton with name__open(), name__load(), and name__attach() instead.
quote: BCC runtime compilation is replaced by a generated skeleton loaded via `name__open()`, `name__load()`, and `name__attach()`.

from_fact: generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach()

subject: user space reading global variables
truth: Stop reading global variables the BCC way; access them via skel->rodata, skel->bss, or skel->data instead.
quote: User space reads global variables via `skel->rodata`, `skel->bss`, or `skel->data`.

subject: bpf_trace_printk
truth: Stop using bpf_trace_printk(); use bpf_printk() instead, which accepts up to 3 arguments.
quote: `bpf_trace_printk()` is replaced by `bpf_printk()` (with a 3-argument limit).

subject: static helper functions
truth: Static helper functions in BPF must be marked static __always_inline, not just static.
quote: Static helper functions must be marked `static __always_inline`.
